seo.title: Building Angular signals and standalone components with A...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Angular signals and standalone components with Angular CLI and RxJS.

content.intro: This guide provides a structured approach to integrating Angular standalone components and signals into existing module-based applications. Focuses on architectural decisions, dependency management, and migration patterns for enterprise environments.
prerequisites: Existing Angular application with NgModule-based architecture
Angular Material or equivalent component library

steps.title: Audit existing NgModule dependencies
steps.explanation: Identify all NgModule imports, declarations, and providers. Use ng generate module --dry-run to list module dependencies. Check for circular dependencies and shared service providers.

code.content:
ng generate module --dry-run shared --flat
ng generate module --dry-run feature --flat

pitfalls: Missing transitive dependencies in lazy-loaded modules
Shared services with module-level providers instead of root

steps.title: Enable standalone component support
steps.explanation: Update angular.json to include standalone: true in the build configuration. Modify main.ts to use bootstrapApplication() instead of platformBrowser().
pitfalls: Forgetting to update all module entry points

steps.title: Convert components to standalone
steps.explanation: Use ng generate component --standalone to create new components. For existing components, manually update metadata to remove NgModule declarations and add imports for dependencies.
pitfalls: Forgetting to add required imports for component dependencies
Mixing standalone and NgModule components in the same module

steps.title: Implement signal-based state management
steps.explanation: Replace @Input() properties with signals using signal() and effect(). Update templates to use signal values directly. Refactor service state management to use signal-based patterns.
pitfalls: Not using the correct signal update syntax
Forgetting to add dependencies to effect() triggers

steps.title: Refactor module providers to root
steps.explanation: Update service providers from NgModule providers to providedIn: 'root'. Use Angular CLI to generate services with root injection. Verify dependency resolution in component constructors.
pitfalls: Legacy services with module-level providers causing multiple instances
Incorrect provider configuration in lazy-loaded modules

steps.title: Implement lazy loading for feature modules
steps.explanation: Configure route loadChildren with function expressions. Use Angular CLI to generate lazy modules. Update app routing module to use loadChildren syntax.
pitfalls: Missing route configuration in parent modules

content.conclusion: After implementing standalone components and signals, verify all dependencies through e2e tests. Monitor bundle size with Angular CLI build analytics. Maintain backward compatibility during phased migration.
